title: selaginella porphyrospora
intro: Selaginella porphyrospora, also known as the "blood fern," is a type of ancient, small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. Traditionally, Selaginella porphyrospora has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant is believed to possess various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing blood sugar levels, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Selaginella porphyrospora is often used in the form of a tea or tincture, and it can also be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
